# RefactorKit v0.3.0 Release Notes

RefactorKit `v0.3.0` publishes the post-beta compiler-backed Java analysis
foundation and integration metadata needed for the path to `v1.0.0`. The release
keeps the documented API baseline at `0.2`; it is not the stable API `1.0` freeze.

## Highlights

- Eclipse JDT is the selected semantic analysis source. Clean binding evidence
  now supports exact type/class-style rename, signed method and annotation-element
  rename, exact field rename, move-type file scoping, safe-delete references, and
  binding-proven unused exact-import removal.
- Signed method rename propagates through complete scanned-source class/interface
  override families and refuses external family members that cannot be changed
  safely.
- Java annotation types and signed annotation elements participate in symbol,
  definition, reference, and supported rename workflows.
- Conventional Maven/Gradle compiled outputs, local `lib`/`libs` JARs, module
  source roots, and generated classpath lists are supplied to JDT without silently
  executing build scripts.
- CLI, daemon, LSP, and MCP expose centralized implementation version metadata;
  daemon `server.capabilities` provides read-only method stability, project/write
  requirements, and safety flags before project open.
- The compatibility/deprecation policy is accepted for API baseline `0.2`, and a
  detailed immutable-RC plan defines the remaining gates to `v1.0.0`.
- Release automation now classifies hyphenated tags as prereleases and stable tags
  as latest non-prerelease releases.

## Safety behavior

- Every workspace mutation remains preview-first and `PatchPlan`-backed.
- Apply verifies the snapshot, rejects overlap and unsafe paths, writes transaction
  metadata, and preserves rollback support.
- JDT authority is granted only to tested clean-evidence slices. Parse/classpath
  problems cause the documented refusal or explicit lexical fallback.
- Same-simple-name types/annotations, unrelated fields/methods, overloads,
  shadowing locals, and unrelated packages are covered by semantic precision
  tests for the promoted slices.

## Artifacts

The release workflow replaces the metadata placeholders below in the published
GitHub Release body with the source tag, release commit, final asset URL, and
SHA-256.

- Source tag: _TBD before release_
- Release commit: _TBD before release_
- Runtime zip: `refactorkit-runtime-0.3.0-linux-x86_64.zip`
  - Final URL: _TBD after release artifact is published_
- Checksum file: `refactorkit-runtime-0.3.0-linux-x86_64.zip.sha256`
  - Final SHA-256: _TBD after release artifact is built_

Verification after download:

```bash
sha256sum -c refactorkit-runtime-0.3.0-linux-x86_64.zip.sha256
unzip refactorkit-runtime-0.3.0-linux-x86_64.zip -d /tmp/refactorkit-v0.3.0

RK=/tmp/refactorkit-v0.3.0/refactorkit/bin/refactorkit

env -u JAVA_HOME "$RK" --version
env -u JAVA_HOME "$RK" --help
env -u JAVA_HOME "$RK" scan samples/java-maven-simple
```

The scan command should be run from a RefactorKit source checkout containing the
`samples/` directory. The runtime itself does not require a globally installed
Java runtime.

## Release-candidate evidence

Completed on the release candidate:

- [x] implementation metadata is `0.3.0`; API metadata remains `0.2`;
- [x] compiler-backed analysis strategy is recorded in ADR 0008;
- [x] version and capability metadata have CLI/daemon/LSP/MCP contract coverage;
- [x] JDT-backed promoted planner slices have precision, fallback, and refusal
      tests;
- [x] golden regression suite contains 22 passing product cases;
- [x] stable-release sequence and acceptance gates are documented;
- [x] release automation derives prerelease/latest classification from the tag.

Required before tagging:

- [ ] branch CI is green on the release commit;
- [x] `./gradlew build` passes from the release candidate;
- [x] `./refactorkit test-golden` reports `22/22`;
- [x] `./gradlew packageCliRuntime distCliRuntimeZip` passes;
- [x] packaged runtime passes `JAVA_HOME`-unset version/help/sample smoke checks;
- [x] release-candidate diff passes `git diff --check`.

Required after publication:

- [ ] GitHub Release is published as a non-prerelease latest release;
- [ ] published body contains injected tag, commit, asset URL, and SHA-256;
- [ ] downloaded checksum verifies the downloaded runtime zip;
- [ ] independently extracted runtime passes the documented smoke checks;
- [ ] local production deployment records the verified release version/hash.

## Known limitations

- JDT `3.37.0` currently parses at Java 21 compliance. The full advertised Java
  8-through-25 source-level matrix is a `v1.0.0` acceptance gate, not a claim of
  this release.
- RefactorKit does not automatically execute Maven or Gradle to resolve dependency
  graphs. Missing classpath evidence can cause explicit fallback or refusal.
- Reflection, arbitrary framework/configuration strings, generated code, and
  annotation-processor outputs require manual review.
- Unsigned method rename may retain lexical behavior and overload warnings. Signed
  method selectors are the authoritative exact method path.
- Extract method and change signature remain limited experimental operations and
  may conservatively refuse unsupported control-flow/signature shapes.
- Wildcard and unresolved imports are preserved by organize imports.
- LSP/MCP surfaces retain their documented beta/experimental classifications;
  stable classification is deferred to API `1.0` acceptance.

## Migration from v0.2.0-beta

- No documented API `0.2` method or command migration is required.
- Consumers should read `server.version`/CLI version metadata and continue using
  `apiVersion=0.2` for compatibility decisions.
- Daemon clients may optionally call `server.capabilities` before `project.open`.
- Preview warnings now distinguish JDT authority from lexical fallback/refusal;
  clients should display those warnings instead of treating all previews as the
  same evidence level.
- Scripts relying on broad unsigned member rename should adopt signed method
  selectors where exact overload identity is required.
